evidence: Sibling packages under src/satnet/ follow three different __init__.py strategies: metrics/ uses eager re-exports with __all__, models/ uses lazy __getattr__ imports, and legacy/ declares __all__ as bare strings without imports. simulation/ and network/ have no __init__.py at all.

Scripts follow two different sys.path manipulation patterns: 6 of 8 scripts use the guarded SRC_PATH pattern (if str(SRC_PATH) not in sys.path), while train_gnn_model.py and visualize_constellation.py use unconditional sys.path.insert with different path computation styles.
train_gnn_model.py is the only script using logging for output; all 7 other scripts use print(). This creates a style island where one sibling follows a completely different output protocol.
Only 2 of 8 scripts (export_design_dataset.py, train_gnn_model.py) accept argparse CLI arguments; the remaining 6 hardcode parameters, making the configurability pattern unpredictable.
Shebang lines appear on train_gnn_model.py and visualize_constellation.py but not on the other 6 scripts.

issues.summary: Five sibling packages use three different __init__.py strategies; two packages omit __init__.py entirely
evidence: metrics/__init__.py uses eager imports and re-exports with __all__ (10 symbols)
models/__init__.py uses lazy __getattr__ with __all__ (3 symbols)
legacy/__init__.py declares __all__ = ['engine', 'failures'] without any imports
simulation/ and network/ have no __init__.py at all, despite being imported as packages by other modules
issues.suggestion: Standardize on one __init__.py strategy across all subpackages. The lazy __getattr__ pattern from models/ is a good choice for packages with heavy dependencies (torch, sklearn). For lightweight packages (metrics, legacy), eager re-exports are fine. At minimum, add __init__.py to simulation/ and network/ for consistency and to make the package boundary explicit.

issues.summary: Scripts use two different sys.path manipulation patterns for importing from src/
evidence: 6 of 8 scripts use the guarded pattern: PROJECT_ROOT/SRC_PATH defined, then 'if str(SRC_PATH) not in sys.path: sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_PATH))'
train_gnn_model.py uses unconditional 'sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT / "src"))' with SCRIPT_DIR instead of PROJECT_ROOT for parent computation
visualize_constellation.py uses a one-liner 'sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "src"))' without any guard or intermediate variables
issues.suggestion: Adopt a single sys.path boilerplate pattern across all scripts — the guarded SRC_PATH pattern used by the majority is the safest (idempotent). Better yet, make the project pip-installable (pyproject.toml with editable install) so scripts can import satnet without path manipulation.

issues.summary: train_gnn_model.py uses logging while all 7 other scripts use print() for user-facing output
evidence: train_gnn_model.py configures logging.basicConfig and uses logger.info/logger.error throughout
All other scripts use print() for progress and result output with no logging setup
A developer copying the output pattern from any other script would use print(), then be surprised by train_gnn_model.py's logging convention
issues.suggestion: Standardize on one output approach for scripts. Since these are CLI tools for human consumption, print() is appropriate for progress and results. If structured logging is desired for all scripts, add a shared logging setup helper and apply it consistently across all 8 scripts.

issues.summary: Only 2 of 8 scripts have shebang lines (#!/usr/bin/env python3)
evidence: train_gnn_model.py and visualize_constellation.py have '#!/usr/bin/env python3' on line 1
The other 6 scripts (export_design_dataset.py, export_failure_dataset.py, failure_sweep.py, score_risk_examples.py, train_design_risk_model.py, train_risk_model.py) omit the shebang
issues.suggestion: Either add shebang lines to all scripts or remove them from the two that have them. Since all scripts are invoked via 'python scripts/...', the shebang is optional but should be consistent.

issues.summary: Only 2 of 8 scripts accept CLI arguments via argparse; the rest hardcode configuration
evidence: export_design_dataset.py and train_gnn_model.py define parse_args() with argparse and accept --num-runs, --seed, --epochs, etc.
export_failure_dataset.py, failure_sweep.py, train_design_risk_model.py, and train_risk_model.py hardcode all parameters (num_runs=100, seed=123, n_estimators=300, etc.) with no CLI configurability
A developer running failure_sweep.py or export_failure_dataset.py cannot adjust parameters without editing source
issues.suggestion: Add argparse to the scripts that hardcode parameters, following the pattern established by export_design_dataset.py. At minimum, expose seed, num_runs, and output paths as CLI arguments.

root_causes: Scripts were likely written at different times by different contributors (or in different LLM sessions), with no shared script template or convention doc to enforce consistency

The __init__.py variation suggests packages were created at different project phases — metrics was early (eager imports), models was optimized later (lazy imports), simulation/network were never given explicit package boundaries
likely_symptoms: inconsistent_shebang_lines is a surface symptom of the deeper script_boilerplate_drift root cause
inconsistent_argparse_usage is partly a maturity symptom — newer scripts got argparse, older ones did not
possible_false_positives: The logging vs print distinction in train_gnn_model.py could be intentional if GNN training was meant to support more structured output for long-running jobs, but this is not documented
